frameworks.name: grid cell vector coding
frameworks.one_line: entorhinal grid cells encode translation vectors between salient stimulus features

frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: saccade-by-saccade evidence accrual confirms or rejects a stimulus-identity hypothesis

frameworks.name: hypothesis-driven constructive perception
frameworks.one_line: directed saccades test predicted next-feature locations

frameworks.name: holistic face processing
frameworks.one_line: model accommodates relational/holistic recognition beyond feature parts

core_question: Can grid cells implement recognition memory for faces, objects, and scenes by encoding inter-feature saccade vectors?
key_assumptions: grid cells fire in visual (eye-movement) space, not only physical space
salient stimulus features serve as anchor landmarks between saccades
an initial identity hypothesis seeds expected next-feature locations
recognition emerges from relative feature layout, not parts alone
manipulations: presence vs absence of grid-cell to occipitotemporal connectivity
saccade trajectories used to traverse feature layouts
key_findings: grid-cell activity variance shows 6-fold symmetry across 360 degrees along saccade trajectories
model recognises familiar faces, objects, and scenes via inter-feature vector sequences
robustness to partial occlusion and to size/position changes
disconnecting grid cells from occipitotemporal inputs produces prosopagnosia-like deficits
interpretations: grid cells provide a neural mechanism for hypothesis-driven, constructive visual recognition
medial temporal lobe contributes to visual memory for relational layout and memory-guided attention
